name: Notify stakeholders on changes with Twilio at scale

parameters.message: =✅ Notify stakeholders on changes with Twilio at scale — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf08524-task`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Probe Endpoint A

### Probe Endpoint A

GET https://service-a.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Probe Endpoint B

GET https://service-b.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Combine Probe Results

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: All Healthy?

### All Healthy?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($json.statusCode || 200) < 400 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Record Uptime Metric · ②→ Already Alerted? (State Check)

### Record Uptime Metric

Appends a new row to 'Task Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Fingerprint for Idempotency · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Already Alerted? (State Check)

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Page On-Call via Slack

### Page On-Call via Slack

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per task (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Task Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Appends a new row to 'Task Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Cap Batch Volume · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Send Summary to Twilio

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Twilio

### Escalate Failure to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Task Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Notify stakeholders on changes with Twilio at scale

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Notify stakeholders on changes with Twilio at scale. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual standups is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Probe Endpoint A → Combine Probe Results → All Healthy? → Record Uptime Metric → Fingerprint for Idempotency → Drop Already-Processed Items → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating standups in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Standardizes the process every time
- Improves response time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

'Already Alerted?' uses cross-execution dedupe specifically so a service that's down for an hour pages on-call once, not on every scheduled probe — alert fatigue is a real failure mode, and this node is the guard against it.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Add a third endpoint to probe and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Point a probe at a URL that returns 500 and confirm exactly one page fires, not one per scheduled check.
3. Change the alert dedupe key so alerts re-fire daily instead of once per incident.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your standups process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
